questions.question: Grandfather had the distinction of being the only member of the party ........ .

options.A: to have bagged any game dead or alive
options.C: to have spotted a deer and wild boar
options.D: to have brought home a monkey

questions.question: Grandfather made straight for the particular cage in which Timothy had been ............. .
What tempted Gandhi to sit in the Persian class ?

options.B: Rigid nature of his Sanskrit teacher
options.C: His love for the Persian language

questions.question: As a student Gandhi was .................. in Arithmetic.
Lencho looked at the north-east sky and foretold about the ensuing rainfall. (Replace the underlined word with another word of same meaning)
Apart from the crest of a low hill, Lencho’s house stood ............... .

options.A: in the middle of the valley
options.B: in the middle of the low valley
options.C: in the middle of a low hill
options.D: in the middle of the low valleys

questions.question: The setting of the poem “The Solitary Reaper” is
What makes William Wordsworth motionless and still ?

options.A: His weariness caused due to climbing up the hill
options.B: His interest for learning the words and rhythm of the song
options.C: His attraction to the thrilling voice of the girl
options.D: His emotional feeling for the melodious voice of the girl

questions.question: The poet considers the ‘Summer Sun’ pleasant because ............... .

options.A: she belongs to Ireland, a cold country
options.B: in summer many kinds of flowers bloom
options.C: in summer streams flow and nature looks lively

questions.question: Dorabji Edulji Gimi was a lenient, popular, good and disciplinarian teacher. (Find the odd one out in the underlined part.)
In the poem ‘Village Song’ the child desires more than the comforts of home. It is ............... .

options.A: a rich life in the city
options.D: a life of art and creativity

questions.question: The flowers mentioned in the poem ‘Village Song’ are ............... .
The river-isle is haunted by ............... .
Which state is separated from the Northeast India by ‘Siliguri Corridor’ ?
Ningol Chakouba, a festival of Manipur ............... .

options.A: binds and revives the relationship between married girls and their parents
options.B: preserves and passes down their oral history
options.C: encourages their cultural and artistic activities
options.D: represents hunting and food gathering activities

questions.question: The North-East India is a collective name for the ..................... of India.
The flowers in the poem ‘The Flower School’ are compared to the ............... .
‘The storm-clouds rumble in the sky.’ Here the word ‘rumble’ means ............... .

options.A: to make a loud harsh sound
options.B: to make a long deep sound

questions.question: The beggar agreed to chop wood because ............... .

options.A: he was hungry and wanted work
options.B: he had been trapped by his own words
options.D: he was in need of money for vodka

questions.question: Sergei, the advocate, suddenly remembered about the beggar when his eyes fell on the man’s ............... .
Day by day, the amounts of harmful gases are increasing in our atmosphere. It would eventually lead to ............... .
Which of the following diseases in the box are caused by air pollution? (I. Eye irritations, II. Scratchy throats, III. Malaria, IV. Respiratory illness)
Brain : I’m Vicky’s brain ! Ammu : Where are you ? Brain : ........................... !
Vicky rode his bike without wearing his helmet to his friend for .............. .
Which one of Vicky’s characteristics is not displayed in the poem, “From the Formalin Jar” ?

options.A: Vicky was a strong and stout young man.
options.B: Vicky believed in taking pain to succeed in life.
options.D: Vicky was good at maths and computer.

questions.question: Lord Eustace Percy advised the students not to ............... .

options.A: choose a trade or profession carefully
options.C: hesitate to ask advice from teachers

questions.question: What is the priceless possession of an individual ?
During which trip did the bullock make a last effort, but fell down on the ground ?
Who has all the following characteristics in the story ‘Village Judge’ ? (* Had a small piece of property. * Threatened to call a Panchayat. * Neglected at old age. * Deep faith in God.)
“Labour was the only thing men called their own.” Who said this statement ?
Pick out the correct grammatical sentence :

options.A: Muna does not neither eat fish nor meat.
options.B: Muna neither does not eat fish nor meat.
options.C: Muna neither eats fish nor meat.
options.D: Muna eats neither fish nor meat.

questions.question: Idli and chutney .................. my favourite breakfast.
My family ..................... divided on the property issue.
My grandmother is ........................ the doctor’s clinic.
This answer was written ............... .
My family visited me last week. The underlined word can be replaced by ............... .
Suhani asked her mother ............... .
My grandfather set ................. a new school for poor children.
Tell me if Smruti would go there. The underlined clause is a / an ............... .
I have already read the story book you gave me. The underlined subordinate clause is a / an ............... .
The boy danced ...................... he were a film hero.

How did you work out the sum ?
It can be changed into Passive voice as : ...................... .

options.A: How was the sum worked out ?
options.B: How the sum was worked out ?
options.C: How is the sum worked out ?
options.D: The sum was worked out by you

questions.explanation: The active voice is in the simple past tense ('did... work out'). In passive voice, this changes to 'was/were + past participle' ('was... worked out'). Since it is a question, the auxiliary verb 'was' must come before the subject 'the sum'.

questions.question: The students laboured hard .................. they were tired.
questions.explanation: 'Although' is a conceding conjunction used to show contrast. It fits perfectly here because studying hard contrasts with being tired.

questions.question:
He said, “Good evening”.

It can be changed into indirect speech as : ............................. .

options.D: He wished me a good evening.

questions.explanation: Salutations like 'Good evening' are reported using the verb 'wish'. When transforming to indirect speech, an implied objective pronoun ('me' or 'them') is naturally introduced to clarify who was greeted.

questions.question: Pick out the correct grammatical sentence :

options.A: There was no news nevertheless she went out.
options.B: There was no news, nevertheless she went out.
options.C: There was no news, nevertheless, she went out.
options.D: There was no news ; nevertheless, she went out.

questions.question: Character is a priceless possession. Keep it, therefore, untarnished. The underlined word can be substituted by ............... .
She said, “When I left home, it began to rain”. Choose the correct reported speech of the above sentence.

options.A: She said that when I left home, it began to rain.
options.B: She said that when she left home, it began to rain.
options.C: She said that when she had left home, it had begun to rain.
options.D: She told that when she had left home, it would begin to rain.

questions.question: The house ...................... two bedrooms, a kitchen and a living room.
A violent tropical storm with very strong winds is called ............... .
There is almost no sugar at home. The underlined words can be replaced by ............... .
